repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
### Problem:
The Lua test harness still ran through standalone `-ll` mode, so tests depended on the low-level Lua path instead of the regular Nvim Lua environment. Moving the harness to `-l` also exposed that `os.exit()` used Lua's raw process exit and bypassed Nvim teardown. That raw exit forced the startup test to disable LeakSanitizer because ASAN/LSAN could interfere with the observed exit code.

### Solution:
Run the harness and related fixtures with `nvim -l`. Patch `os.exit()` in the main Lua state to exit through `getout()`, so `-l` scripts observe normal Nvim shutdown while standalone `-ll` remains available for generator-style scripts. As a consequence, the startup test can assert `os.exit()` without disabling leak detection.

---

Closes #30483
Closes #39116

comment_data.body: this reminds me, we might need to undefine `$NVIM` in various places where the runner starts new child processes.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -27,9 +27,15 @@ end
 local root = repo_root()
 prepend_package_roots({ root, root .. '/test', '.', './test' })
 
+-- The harness is not an Nvim instance under test. If its startup server stays
+-- visible, serverlist({ peer = true }) can connect back to the runner and wait
+-- forever for an RPC response.
+if vim.v.servername ~= '' then

comment_data.body:
is TSAN/ASAN checking the test runner `nvim`? maybe we can disable TSAN/ASAN for the test runner?

or maybe it's useful, since this looks legitimate:

```
= ==21782==ERROR: ThreadSanitizer: SEGV on unknown address 0x000000000000 (pc 0x55bc933dc148 bp 0x7fff3d556320 sp 0x7fff3d556300 T21782)
= ==21782==The signal is caused by a READ memory access.
= ==21782==Hint: address points to the zero page.
=     #0 luv_cleanup_req /home/runner/work/neovim/neovim/.deps/build/src/luv/src/lreq.c:72:42 (nvim+0x8e1148) (BuildId: 4a7f9497b759ed6a84c282b37692b824b25de1ea)
=     #1 luv_fs_gc /home/runner/work/neovim/neovim/.deps/build/src/luv/src/fs.c:43:3 (nvim+0x8e56cd) (BuildId: 4a7f9497b759ed6a84c282b37692b824b25de1ea)
```

perhaps `check_logs()` could show an improved message like `This trace is from the test runner itself.`

comment_data.body:
To change the log for the test runner, I think you need something like:

```diff
diff --git a/cmake/RunTests.cmake b/cmake/RunTests.cmake
index 20063116dd..44c6398ee5 100644
--- a/cmake/RunTests.cmake
+++ b/cmake/RunTests.cmake
@@ -71,6 +71,9 @@ endif()
 # TMPDIR: for testutil.tmpname() and Nvim tempname().
 set(ENV{TMPDIR} "${BUILD_DIR}/Xtest_tmpdir${TEST_SUFFIX}")
 file(MAKE_DIRECTORY $ENV{TMPDIR})
+set(TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE "${BUILD_DIR}/Xtest_runner${TEST_SUFFIX}.pid")
+set(ENV{NVIM_TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE} "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}")
+file(REMOVE "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}")
 
 # HISTFILE: do not write into user's ~/.bash_history
 set(ENV{HISTFILE} "/dev/null")
@@ -116,6 +119,22 @@ endmacro()
 
 if(res)
   message(STATUS "Tests exited non-zero: ${res}")
+  if(DEFINED ENV{LOG_DIR} AND IS_DIRECTORY "$ENV{LOG_DIR}" AND EXISTS "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}")
+    file(READ "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}" TEST_RUNNER_PID)
+    string(STRIP "${TEST_RUNNER_PID}" TEST_RUNNER_PID)
+    if(NOT TEST_RUNNER_PID STREQUAL "")
+      file(GLOB TEST_RUNNER_SANITIZER_LOGS "$ENV{LOG_DIR}/*san.${TEST_RUNNER_PID}")
+      if(TEST_RUNNER_SANITIZER_LOGS)
+        message(
+          STATUS
+          "Sanitizer report(s) came from the Lua test runner (${NVIM_PRG} -l), not a child Nvim test session:"
+        )
+        foreach(log IN LISTS TEST_RUNNER_SANITIZER_LOGS)
+          message(STATUS "  ${log}")
+        endforeach()
+      endif()
+    endif()
+  endif()
 
   # Dump the logfile on CI (if not displayed and moved already).
   if(CI_BUILD)
@@ -124,9 +143,12 @@ if(res)
     endif()
   endif()
 
+  file(REMOVE "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}")
   message(FATAL_ERROR "${TEST_TYPE} tests failed with error: ${res}")
 endif()
 
+file(REMOVE "${TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE}")
+
 if(CI_BUILD)
   file(SIZE $ENV{NVIM_LOG_FILE} FILE_SIZE)
   if(NOT ${FILE_SIZE} MATCHES "^0$")
diff --git a/test/runner.lua b/test/runner.lua
index b434351384..3305503d62 100644
--- a/test/runner.lua
+++ b/test/runner.lua
@@ -27,6 +27,14 @@ end
 local root = repo_root()
 prepend_package_roots({ root, root .. '/test', '.', './test' })
 
+local runner_pid_file = os.getenv('NVIM_TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE')
+if runner_pid_file and runner_pid_file ~= '' then
+  local f = assert(io.open(runner_pid_file, 'w'))
+  f:write(tostring(uv.os_getpid()), '\n')
+  f:close()
+  uv.os_unsetenv('NVIM_TEST_RUNNER_PID_FILE')
+end
+
 -- The harness is not an Nvim instance under test. If its startup server stays
 -- visible, serverlist({ peer = true }) can connect back to the runner and wait
 -- forever for an RPC response.
```

Which is a bit ugly. Wdyt?

But maybe separate PR?
